name: The Citizens Foundation USA
mission:
The Citizens Foundation, USA (TCF-USA) is a professionally managed tax-exempt charity, with a support network of volunteer chapters across the USA. We support the building of civil society in Pakistan by providing quality education to underprivileged children, promoting gender equality & partnering for reform of public education. We believe that education is a basic human right. Universal access to education is a prerequisite for democratic institutions to flourish in Pakistan and for its people to compete in a global economy.
TCF currently has 1,833 schools in all the provinces of Pakistan with 280,000 children enrolled, nearly half of them girls. TCF has trained and employed 13,000 women as teachers and principals, making it the largest employer of women in the private sector.
TCF also provides scholarships for tertiary education to the deserving graduates of its' secondary schools and an adult literacy program that has transformed the lives of women.
theoryOfChange: The Citizens Foundation USA (TCF-USA) has a documented approach to measuring change through its Quality Assurance (QA) team, as described on its 'Education Program' page. This team ensures a high quality of education by monitoring education standards, conducting school visits, formal classroom observations, and collecting insights from principals, teachers, and students. Based on these assessments, school improvement plans are devised, and results and performances are measured and analyzed to identify areas for improvement for future strategy and action. The QA team also conducts two centralized examinations (Midterms and Finals) in an academic year, preparing examination papers, monitoring marking, and compiling results. This structured approach ensures that an acceptable education standard is maintained across all TCF school units.

all_citations.claim: The charity received a Charity Navigator score of 99.33333333333333/100 and maintains high financial accountability.
The organization is zakat-eligible and follows specific asnaf categories for distribution.
Total revenue reached $21,703,144 with a program expense ratio of 89.5%.
The organization provides operational support for schools, constructs new units, and offers higher education scholarships.
TCF-USA has successfully converted schools to solar power and provided higher education scholarships.
The organization tracks and reports on school unit capacity as a core metric.
The organization offers multiple donation options and giving methods for supporters.
TCF-USA maintains an active volunteer program to engage the community in its mission.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: With an overall score of 87/100, TCF-USA demonstrates high impact through direct service delivery and excellent financial stewardship, making it a highly reliable choice for donors.
areas_for_improvement: Working capital reserves are currently at 4.7 months, which is below the common nonprofit benchmark of 6-12 months for long-term sustainability.
Expanding the geographic diversity of programs beyond the current focus areas in Pakistan to reach more underprivileged populations.

dimension_explanations.alignment: TCF-USA shows strong alignment with donor interests, particularly for those seeking high-urgency global education causes and zakat-eligible opportunities [2][8]. The organization has a proven track record of increasing school unit capacity and fostering volunteer engagement to support its mission [7][9].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization achieves a high impact score of 49/50 by maintaining a low cost per beneficiary of approximately $55.37 and a high program expense ratio of 89.5% [1][3]. Its direct service model focuses on building new school units, providing higher education scholarships, and converting existing schools to solar energy for sustainability [4][5].

baseline_narrative.headline: The Citizens Foundation USA empowers underprivileged children in Pakistan through high-quality education and sustainable school infrastructure.
strengths: Exceptional program expense ratio of 89.5%, ensuring the vast majority of funds directly support educational initiatives.
High fundraising efficiency with a cost of only $0.09 per $1 raised.
Strong financial health and accountability as evidenced by a Charity Navigator score of 99.33333333333333/100.
baseline_narrative.summary: The Citizens Foundation USA (TCF-USA) supports the development of civil society in Pakistan by providing quality education to marginalized youth through school construction and operational support [4]. The organization maintains a high level of financial transparency and is recognized as zakat-eligible, allowing donors to fulfill their religious obligations through educational empowerment [2].

alignment.explanation: TCF-USA is a strong fit for Muslim donors, offering <cite id="5">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and serving <cite id="6">zakat-eligible asnaf</cite> in a Muslim-majority country.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust TCF-USA due to its <cite id="13">Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite> and <cite id="12">99.99.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite>. The organization maintains a <cite id="16">fully independent 15-member board</cite> and undergoes <cite id="21">regular financial audits</cite>.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization could publish more detailed third-party impact evaluations beyond internal metrics.

impact.explanation: With a <cite id="4">89.5% program ratio</cite> and a low <cite id="15">$55.37 cost per beneficiary</cite>, TCF-USA is highly efficient. They track <cite id="22">four distinct outcome metrics</cite> through Candid to validate their educational results.
impact.improvement: Impact measurement could be strengthened by <cite id="23">expanding services to geographies</cite> with even more limited nonprofit coverage.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to fulfill their zakat through a highly efficient, large-scale educational institution with a proven track record in Pakistan.
donor_motivations: Want to ensure nearly 100% of their donation reaches the field
Prioritize educational empowerment as a form of long-term Sadaqah Jariyah
Value high transparency and third-party validation (Charity Navigator/Candid)
Focus on supporting Muslim-majority communities in South Asia
giving_considerations: Donations support a large, established network rather than a small startup
The organization is highly specialized in the Pakistani context
Revenue has seen a slight downward trend recently
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking to fund advocacy, policy reform, or educational initiatives outside of Pakistan.

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: The organization tracks <cite id="22">four outcome metrics</cite> and provides detailed annual reporting on school units and scholarships.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: TCF-USA aims to <cite id="24">remove barriers of class and privilege</cite> by providing quality education to underprivileged children, turning them into <cite id="24">agents of positive change</cite>.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that school construction translates into actual learning outcomes and student advancement.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="10">17 new school units constructed</cite> recently and a massive waiting list for scholarships, the organization can absorb significant additional capital.

peer_comparison.differentiator: TCF-USA's <cite id="4">89.5% program ratio</cite> is significantly higher than the <cite id="11">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.

strengths.detail: The charity directs a remarkable <cite id="4">89.5% of its expenses toward programs</cite>, significantly exceeding the <cite id="11">peer median of 89.6%</cite>.
TCF-USA holds an <cite id="12">overall score of 99.99.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite> and has earned the <cite id="13">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>.
The organization demonstrates high efficiency, spending only <cite id="14">$0.10 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.

strengths_deep_dive: TCF-USA's impact is rooted in its direct service delivery model, which achieves a <cite id="15">cost per beneficiary of approximately $55.37</cite>, a figure considered exceptional for the global education sector. Their commitment to sustainability is evidenced by <cite id="7">converting 8 schools to solar energy</cite> in a single year to reduce operational costs and environmental impact. Furthermore, the organization maintains <cite id="16">100% independent board members</cite>, ensuring rigorous oversight and governance of its <cite id="17">$18 million in annual contributions</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: The Citizens Foundation USA (TCF-USA) is a premier educational non-profit dedicated to <cite id="1">building civil society in Pakistan</cite> by providing high-quality education to underprivileged children. Since its <cite id="2">founding in 1995</cite>, the organization has established a massive infrastructure to remove barriers of class and privilege. TCF-USA operates with exceptional financial efficiency, reporting <cite id="3">$18,303,365 in total revenue</cite> for the most recent fiscal year while maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 89.5%</cite>. This ensures that nearly every dollar donated is funneled directly into school operations and student support. The organization is <cite id="5">verified as zakat-eligible</cite>, specifically serving the <cite id="6">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> through its educational programs. Beyond basic schooling, TCF-USA has expanded its impact by <cite id="7">converting schools to solar power</cite> and providing <cite id="8">higher education scholarships</cite> to ensure long-term student success. Their model is highly scalable, currently providing <cite id="9">operational support for 534 schools</cite> and continuing to grow through the <cite id="10">construction of new school units</cite>.
